title: vincetoxicum hirundinaria subsp. nivale
intro: Vincetoxicum hirundinaria subsp. nivale, also known as white swallowwort, is a subspecies of perennial plant in the Apocynaceae family, native to mountainous regions of Europe. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral effects, making it useful in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of white swallowwort include reducing fever, alleviating symptoms of respiratory infections, and supporting the immune system. The plant can be prepared as a herbal infusion, tincture, or dried extract, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
